title: Bartender

rationales.taskAutomation: Drink-making, pacing a bar, and reading a room are dexterity- and judgment-heavy; only tab management and simple pour tracking are automatable.
rationales.toolMaturity: Robotic bartending kiosks exist in a handful of novelty venues and casinos but have not moved into mainstream bar tooling.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is confined to isolated novelty installations; the vast majority of bars run entirely on human bartenders.
rationales.agenticExposure: Tab tracking and drink-order queuing are agent-ownable slices; mixing, pacing, and the social role of the bar are not.

tasksAtRisk: Standard pour measurement for high-volume wells
Basic drink-order queuing at high-traffic events
tasksGrowing: Craft cocktail development and flavor pairing
Reading the room and pacing service responsibly
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 35-3011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Craft cocktail development and flavor innovation
POS and tab-automation fluency for high-volume service

insight: Robotic bar kiosks are a novelty act, not a threat: mixing well drinks at volume while reading a room's mood and pace is still squarely human work. The real automation is in the back end, tab tracking and pour reconciliation, not behind the bar.
